subject: english
learning_objectives: Recognise stressed and unstressed syllables in words

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: A syllable is a unit of pronunciation uttered with one breath effort.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content: The note gives examples of one-syllable, two-syllable, three-syllable, and four-syllable words.

sections.heading: Stress Within Words
sections.content: In words with two or more syllables, one syllable may be pronounced with more force, length, and pitch. That syllable is the stressed syllable.
